_comment: This file is an annotated example of .ghostrun/config.json — the workspace-level configuration file GhostRun reads on startup. Copy it to .ghostrun/config.json in your project and remove the '_comment' keys before committing.

project._comment: Human-readable project name shown in reports and step summaries.
project.name: my-project

_comment_interactionMode: Controls how much GhostRun can act autonomously. 'assist' (default) asks for confirmation before applying AI-generated repairs or flow mutations. 'auto' applies approved actions without prompting — suitable for CI. 'readonly' disables all write operations.

crawlee._comment_crawlee: Set to true to enable Crawlee-based deep crawl recording. Requires additional system dependencies.

ai._comment: AI provider configuration. GhostRun uses Claude (via Anthropic) for auto-repair, flow summarisation, and knowledge extraction.
ai._comment_provider: AI backend to use. 'auto' selects the best available provider based on environment variables present. Other valid values: 'anthropic', 'none' (disables all AI features).
ai._comment_trackUsage: When true, GhostRun logs token counts to .ghostrun/reports/ so you can monitor API spend.
ai._comment_storeSanitizedTranscripts: When true, AI interaction transcripts (with secrets redacted) are stored under .ghostrun/ai/ for debugging and auditing.

policies._comment: Guardrails that control which automated actions GhostRun is permitted to take without explicit operator approval.
policies._comment_allowAutoRepairApply: When false (recommended for human-supervised environments), GhostRun proposes auto-repairs but waits for operator confirmation before writing changes to disk. Set to true in fully automated CI pipelines where unattended repair is acceptable.
policies._comment_allowAiInCi: Governs AI feature usage when the --ci flag is active. 'summary-only' (default) allows AI to generate run summaries but blocks expensive repair calls. 'full' enables all AI features in CI. 'none' disables AI entirely in CI mode.
policies._comment_requireApprovalForFlowMutation: When true, any operation that would modify a flow file (.ghostrun/flows/**) must be explicitly confirmed by the operator. Prevents accidental flow rewrites during interactive sessions.
policies._comment_requireApprovalForSecretUse: When true, GhostRun prompts the operator before injecting a secret from the keychain or environment into a running flow. Useful when auditing is required.
policies._comment_autoImproveEnabled: When true, GhostRun will attempt to iteratively improve failing flows using AI after a run. Disabled by default because it can produce unexpected flow changes; enable deliberately in development environments.
policies._comment_maxAutoImproveIterations: Maximum number of AI-driven improvement cycles per run when autoImproveEnabled is true. Prevents runaway token spend on persistently broken flows.
policies._comment_maxRepairAttemptsPerRun: Maximum number of times GhostRun will attempt to auto-repair a single failing step per run. Increasing this gives the AI more attempts but also increases execution time and token usage.
policies._comment_maxSameFailureRepeats: If the same failure signature is observed this many times in a single run, GhostRun aborts early rather than retrying indefinitely. Prevents long CI runs from hanging on a systemic error.

integrations._comment: Optional work-tracker bridges (issue creation ships in v2.0-alpha).

_comment_activeProfile: The environment profile loaded by default when no --profile flag is passed. Must match a filename (without .json) under .ghostrun/profiles/.
